claims.summary: A dated Razer compatibility-list snapshot identifies candidate model names but grants no transport or RGB authority.
limitations: No candidate PID, carrier map, command semantics, or writable capability is inferred from catalog membership.

claims.summary: All thirteen mouse presentation indexes were physically matched to receiver carriers, including the wheel and logo correction.
limitations: The mapping applies only to paired PID 0x00cd through the reviewed HyperFlux V2 protocol.

claims.summary: Passive reports provide generation-bound mouse identity, contact, connection evidence, and bounded battery telemetry.
limitations: Missing or stale evidence remains unknown; contact does not prove physical power or sleep.

claims.summary: The complete 6-by-17 role-local keyboard transport plane and representative GUI-selected keys were physically verified.
limitations: The transport map does not qualify another PID or another physical keyboard-layout variant.

claims.summary: Passive reports provide generation-bound keyboard identity, connection evidence, and bounded battery telemetry.
limitations: External USB power does not establish a direct USB data route, battery capacity, or charging semantics.

claims.summary: Complete and repeated RGB black requests made both qualified children visibly unlit without semantic-control regressions.
limitations: Visible black is an RGB value, not proof of firmware lighting-off or physical sleep.

claims.summary: Application-produced Direct frames continued across rescan, controller return, receiver renewal, and measured resume.
limitations: Effects are produced by OpenRGB, not by HyperFlux or receiver firmware; exact animation phase is not preserved.

claims.summary: Direct and Static brightness endpoints, explicit Off, and controller-local restoration were physically verified for both children.
limitations: Intermediate brightness linearity and firmware effects are not qualified.

claims.summary: Pinned OpenRGB metadata supplies exact model presentation and keyboard geometry without becoming identity or transport authority.
limitations: Upstream presentation cannot authorize a receiver command or qualify an unknown child.

claims.summary: Receiver reconnect creates a newer generation, invalidates old state, reacquires paired PIDs, and preserves ordinary controls.
limitations: No generation, session, health, or restore claim survives physical receiver removal.

claims.summary: An installed package restored two stable child requests after service startup, receiver renewal, and measured system resume.
limitations: Device-wake restoration and speculative retry are not qualified; only retained stable states are restored.

claims.summary: The Hard Edition surface identity and its firmware-owned charging/status indicator are reviewed observations, not a USB identity or writable RGB surface.
limitations: The surface has no independently qualified host-addressable RGB target and no invented product ID.
